candidates.name: Michelle Lujan Grisham
candidates.notes: 32nd Governor of New Mexico (2019-). Catholic. First Democratic Latina governor in U.S. history. Signed sweeping gun control measures including temporary suspension of concealed carry in Albuquerque (controversial). Signed legislation codifying abortion rights. Pro-immigration. Former U.S. House member.

profile.education: University of New Mexico (BA, University Studies); University of New Mexico School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and health administrator; U.S. Representative (2013-2019); became 32nd governor in 2019.

candidates.name: Martin Heinrich
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from New Mexico (2013-). Raised Lutheran in Cole Camp, Missouri. Heritage Action lifetime ~3%. NRA F. Former U.S. House member (2009-2013). Progressive Democrat. Focuses on clean energy, public lands, and technology policy. Supports gun control, abortion access, immigration reform.

profile.background: Mechanical engineer and former Albuquerque City Councilman. Former U.S. House member. Worked as field engineer and natural resources trustee for New Mexico.

candidates.name: Ben Ray Lujan
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from New Mexico (2021-). Roman Catholic. Heritage Action lifetime ~4%. NRA F. Former U.S. House member (2009-2021) and Assistant Speaker. Self-described progressive Democrat. Suffered stroke in January 2022, returned to Senate. Catholic but votes against Church teaching on abortion and marriage. Term ends January 2027.

profile.background: Former U.S. House member and Assistant Speaker. Former New Mexico Public Regulation Commissioner. Son of longtime state House Speaker Ben Lujan. Worked as engineer and IT professional.

candidates.name: Tara Lujan
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from New Mexico (2021-). Roman Catholic. Heritage Action lifetime ~4%. NRA F. Former U.S. House member (2009-2021) and Assistant Speaker. Self-described progressive Democrat. Suffered stroke in January 2022, returned to Senate. Catholic but votes against Church teaching on abortion and marriage. Term ends January 2027.

profile.background: Former U.S. House member and Assistant Speaker. Former New Mexico Public Regulation Commissioner. Son of longtime state House Speaker Ben Lujan. Worked as engineer and IT professional.
